channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Unveiling the molecular mechanism of Qingwen Baidu decoction against dengue fever: an integrated study of bioinformatic analysis, machine learning and network pharmacology
item.description: This study employed bioinformatic analysis, machine learning, and network pharmacology to elucidate the molecular mechanisms by which Qingwen Baidu decoction, a traditional herbal formulation, exerts activity against dengue fever. The integrated computational approach identified key molecular target

item.title: STAT+: A sweeping new AI to detect heart conditions is coming to OpenEvidence
item.description: EchoNext, a new AI tool cleared by the FDA, will let doctors use an EKG to easily screen patients for structural heart diseases.

item.title: Will AI talent departures hurt alphabet's long-term growth?
item.description: Alphabet shares recorded their steepest one-day decline in more than a year after another prominent AI researcher departed Google DeepMind. The selloff wiped out over $225 billion in market value as investors questioned the company's ability to retain top art…

item.title: Efficient generation of epitope-targeted antibodies with germinal
item.description: Germinal achieves epitope-targeted, de novo complementarity-determining region design with high experimental success rates.

item.title: UN urges AI firms to come clean on their environmental costs
item.description: The United Nations wants artificial-intelligence companies to stop treating the environmental bill as somebody else’s problem. In a call amplified this week by Secretary-General Antonio Guterres, the organisation is pressing the firms behind the AI boom to di…

item.title: Greece opens public consultation on national framework for regulating AI under new EU law
item.description: The draft legislation sets out Greece’s national system for implementing the EU Artificial Intelligence Act, including oversight bodies, complaint procedures, penalties and a regulatory sandbox for start-ups and SMEs The post Greece opens public consultation …

item.title: Bright evening light tied to macular degeneration, glaucoma, and cataracts
item.description: In a massive study of more than 80,000 adults, scientists have found a link between bright artificial light in the evenings and age-related eye disease. At the extreme, light exposure was associated with a 31% higher risk of age-related macular degeneration (…

item.title: Fiftieth anniversary conference marking the 1976 drought
item.description: Environment Agency Chief Executive Philip Duffy’s keynote address looking at how the devastating drought changed the way we manage and use water.

item.title: Five eyes spooks warn AI means infosec incidents can become ‘major operational and financial crises’
item.description: Bosses told to step up and get cybersecurity right

item.title: India and china are home to 2.9 billion people – and together they bought just 13 million pcs in q1
item.description: PLUS: Indian telco ponders broadband satellites; Samsung goes all-in on OpenAI; Vietnam centrally plans ten tech giants; and more!

item.title: Meta launches cheaper smart glasses without ray-ban
item.description: For the past three years, "Meta" and "Ray-Ban" have been synonymous in the smart glasses space. Not anymore. Yesterday, I slipped on several pairs of Meta Glasses - no Ray-Bans - in three different styles and seven colors. One style, I was told several times …

item.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI | techcrunch
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA reforming clinical trials, a Pfizer setback, and much more news
item.description: The latest pharmaceutical news from the Pharmalot campus via the Pharmalittle newsletter

item.title: STAT+: OpenEvidence will add FDA-cleared AI to detect heart disease
item.description: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: OpenEvidence adds AI to detect heart disease, and Cadence raises $100 million, and more.

item.title: HHS has sent drug for Ebola clinical trial
item.description: FDA's early-stage clinical trial program, a mysterious single patient gets retatrutide access, and more health news from Morning Rounds

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude Tag brings an always-on AI teammate to Slack. But beyond productivity, the feature is a strategic play to capture organizational context, institutional knowledge, and enterprise workflows.

item.title: Pre-market clinical investigation approval pathways and the role of early feasibility studies for medical devices: a scoping review to guide EU policy
item.description: This scoping review examines pre-market clinical investigation approval pathways for medical devices in the EU, with particular focus on the role and implementation of early feasibility studies to inform European regulatory policy development.

item.title: STAT+: Definium LSD therapy helped patients with major depression in late-stage trial
item.description: Definium Therapeutics said that its LSD therapy helped patients with major depression in its first Phase 3 trial, bringing the psychedelic drug closer to a potential approval.

item.title: Opinion: STAT+: Enough, already: the problem with clinical trial data collection
item.description: “Nearly 30% of the data collected in clinical trials do not directly inform key decisions, yet patients are still asked to provide it.”

item.title: Cw@60: on longevity – a career spanning 60 years of modern computing
item.description: On 22 September 2026, Computer Weekly turns 60. To mark the milestone, we asked some of our friends - experts, trusted contacts, IT leaders and suppliers - for their perspectives on how tech has changed their lives over six decades

item.title: The future of artificial intelligence in business operations and workplace efficiency
item.description: Artificial Intelligence in Business Operations is rapidly transforming how organizations function, compete, and innovate. From automating repetitive tasks to enabling data-driven decision-making, AI is reshaping the modern business landscape. As technology co…

item.title: Navigating the AI access control minefield
item.description: Rather like the early days of e-commerce, everyone seems to be ‘doing artificial intelligence’. IT leaders must now ensure these systems have secure access to enterprise data

item.title: Which data management costs should survive budget cuts?
item.description: Tighter budgets force hard choices about data governance. This breakdown of essential data management costs shows where to cut spending and where cuts create risk.

item.title: Malaysia to adopt a gradual approach to autonomous driving, to build regulatory, infra aspects first – nanta
item.description: Malaysia will take a gradual, safety-first approach to autonomous driving by building the necessary regulatory and infrastructure foundation for the technology before any large-scale deployment comes about. According to works minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nan…

item.title: Non-coding variant prioritization based on cell type, developmental stage and evolutionary constraint
item.description: By using deep learning sequence models, we predict non-coding variant effects across the allele frequency spectrum in over 100 fetal and adult cell types. Linking these data with evolutionary constraint helps prioritize variants across conditions, such as Alz…

item.title: Biological aging and generational shifts in early-onset cancer risk
item.description: Analyses of population cohorts found that young adults exhibited earlier systemic and organ-specific aging, which was associated with increased risk of early-onset cancer compared with older adults born decades earlier.

item.title: Collaborative care intervention for risky opioid use among primary care patients: The STOP randomized clinical trial
item.description: The STOP trial is a randomized clinical trial evaluating a collaborative care intervention designed to address risky opioid use in primary care patients. The intervention leverages a team-based approach involving primary care providers and addiction specialists to improve patient outcomes for opioid

item.title: Development of phenotype algorithms for the detection of adverse events in electronic health record data: a multicentre study
item.description: This multicentre study developed phenotype algorithms to automatically detect adverse events within electronic health record data. The research represents a collaborative effort to improve pharmacovigilance through systematic identification of drug-related safety signals in routine clinical data.

item.title: STAT+: Closely watched Pfizer lung cancer drug falls short in clinical trial
item.description: Pfizer said an experimental drug it hoped could replace a widely used chemotherapy in one of the most common forms of lung cancer fell short in a clinical trial.

item.title: STAT+: FDA to launch pilot program to speed up early-stage clinical trials
item.description: Federal health officials aim to speed up drug development and better compete with China.

item.title: STAT+: Another big deal, another sign biotech M&A is back
item.description: AbbVie buys Apogee Therapeutics, LSD therapy Phase 3 results, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: STAT+: Despite transplant referrals, most kidney patients don’t make the waitlist
item.description: An NIH grant proposal poses new threats, more cases of a livestock disease in Europe, and more health news from Morning Rounds

item.title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
item.description: Google DeepMind and A24 are teaming up to build AI filmmaking tools.

item.title: Three things to watch amid Anthropic’s latest feud with the government
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. For those of you enjoying your summer unaware of Anthropic’s latest feud with the US government, here’s a recap: In April the company said it had built an AI mode

item.title: How Anthropic may have talked itself into an AI export ban
item.description: The company warned about dangers of advanced AI far more than rival OpenAI.

item.title: Import AI 462: Superpersuasion; self-sustaining AI; paths to ASI
item.description: Welcome to Import AI, a newsletter about AI research. Import AI runs on arXiv, cappuccinos, and feedback from readers. If you’d like to support this, please subscribe. Subscribe now AI can decisively out-persuade humans:…“AI systems were reliably more persuasive than expert humans”…Researchers

item.title: Alphabet shares drop after second AI star departs for rival
item.description: Alphabet Inc. shares tumbled on Monday following the departure of another high-profile artificial intelligence leader to a rival. Read here

item.title: Alphabet paces for worst day in a year on AI concerns after high-profile exits
item.description: Google parent Alphabet has seen consecutive high-profile artificial intelligence researchers leave.

item.title: Deadly proteins behind mad cow disease might help Us fight superbugs
item.description: The same prions responsible for mad cow disease and other deadly brain disorders could contain fragments that can treat drug-resistant bacteria, new research suggests.

item.title: Tcrbinder: unified pre-trained language model with paired-chain synergy for predicting t-cell receptor binding specificity
item.description: Author summary T-cell receptors (TCRs) play a central role in adaptive immunity by recognizing peptide-HLA complexes, but predicting their binding specificity has remained a formidable challenge due to sequence diversity and the cooperative action of α and β …

item.title: Beyond the canonical: the role of post-transcriptional regulation in drug-target interaction prediction
item.description: Author summary Computational drug–target interaction models have become central tools in modern drug discovery, yet they typically represent each gene with a single canonical protein sequence, overlooking the fact that most human genes produce multiple isofor…

item.title: From materials simulation to experimental astronomy, new nvidia AI software unlocks scientific discoveries
item.description: At the ISC conference running in Hamburg this week, NVIDIA is introducing new software that speeds AI for science, from chemistry and materials discovery to the search for dark matter. The NVIDIA DAQIRI library and new NVIDIA ALCHEMI NIM microservices — as w…

item.title: Nvidia vera rubin delivers world-class supercomputers for science
item.description: With 7 Exaflops of AI for Science and 5 Petaflops of Native FP64 Performance, Vera Rubin Packs TOP500 Supercomputing in a Single RackNews Summary: NVIDIA Vera Rubin accelerates the world's ...

item.title: Anthropic’s new privacy policy collects biometric data from flagged claude users
item.description: Anthropic has updated its privacy policy to allow the company to require some Claude users to upload government-issued identification and submit selfie photos or videos for identity verification. The updated policy, which takes effect on July 8, introduces a …

item.title: Ireland takes the EU presidency with big tech paying 40 per cent of its tax bill
item.description: Ireland takes over the rotating presidency of the Council of the EU on 1 July, inheriting a legislative agenda that includes proposals to curb Europe’s reliance on American tech, simplify the bloc’s digital rulebook, decide whether to ban children from social…

item.title: Anthropic’s mythos AI broke into almost all nsa classified systems in hours
item.description: Senate testimony claims Anthropic’s Mythos AI breached NSA and Cyber Command systems in hours, prompting a U.S.-ordered shutdown. On June 12, the Trump administration directed Anthropic to restrict access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5, its two most capable models, …

item.title: Airia named as a visionary in the 2026 gartner® magic quadrant™ for AI governance platforms
item.description: For us, this recognition validates Airia’s unified approach to AI security and governance with furthest position on Completeness of Vision axis ATLANTA, June 22, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Airia, the AI control plane for enterprise organizations, today announced…

item.title: Nvidia introduces halos for robotics to bridge the physical AI safety gap
item.description: Nivida Corp. today announced Halos for Robotics, the industry’s first full framework for robotic safety systems that encompasses building, testing and managing complete artificial intelligence robotics applications. Automation has been part of industrial and …

item.title: Bioaffinity technologies reports positive preclinical findings showing proprietary gene silencing technology is selectively cytotoxic to squamous skin cancer cells without harming healthy cells
item.description: SAN ANTONIO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Jun 22, 2026-- bioAffinity Technologies, Inc.(Nasdaq: BIAF; BIAFW), a biotechnology company advancing early-stage cancer diagnostics and targeted therapeutics, will present preclinical findings for a novel therapeutic approach th…

item.title: A lactylation- and autophagy-associated prognostic signature reveals lsec-derived clec3b as a novel mediator of hepatocellular carcinoma suppression
item.description: Author summary Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) is a major global health challenge with limited treatment options and poor prognosis for advanced cases. The tumor microenvironment, particularly the intricate communication between liver cells and surrounding sup…
